recent_activities_14d.subject: Position Merlin AI (Merlin Intake for centralized request handling, Agentic AI for supplier negotiation and invoice OCR

our_open_deliverables.text: Confirm and communicate the available configuration options for setting thresholds or category-based rules for two-way versus three-way matching to Richard Morin.
Send the SAP integration partner level details (or link) to Richard Morin as requested.
Follow up with Perdue's procurement team (Neil Schloss and others) regarding next steps in the evaluation and down-select process and share additional follow-up materials.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Perdue to provide the list of current manual invoice validation processes to Zycus for review and confirmation.
Perdue to reconnect with Zycus after vendor assessment (approximately 30 days from 18 Jun, mid-July 2026 expected) and share down-select results and next steps.

competitive_position.summary: Agiloft CLM and Maximo P2P are confirmed in the field. Agiloft replaced Zycus iContract 3 years ago and owns the CLM relationship; Maximo is part of Perdue's tech stack. The broader S2P competitive set (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, or Oracle) is unknown, creating blind-spot risk in a multi-vendor RFI. Zycus's wedge: AI/Merlin differentiation and seamless Agiloft integration (protect their CLM investment while filling S2P gaps). However, without scorecard intelligence or competitive standing, Zycus cannot activate the wedge effectively.

competitors.name: Agiloft CLM
competitors.quote: Perdue replaced Zycus iContract with Agiloft CLM 3 years ago (known risk from prior sweep memory). No recent call or Salesforce mention, but Agiloft owns the CLM relationship and may defend or expand.

competitors.name: Maximo P2P
competitors.quote: Maximo P2P integration referenced as a requirement during 21 May demo.

champion_strength.source: SF Next Step 20 May, 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun; SF Tasks 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun; demo call notes 21 May
champion_strength.summary: Neil Schloss is the identified champion, but his advocacy is developing rather than strong. He is responsive, transparent, and facilitating the RFI process, but there is no evidence of internal selling, scorecard influence, competitive blocking, or access to the Economic Buyer (Richard Morin). His role appears to be process coordinator rather than advocate. Strength: developing. At risk: no, but weak. If Neil lacks influence in the down-select, Zycus may lack an internal advocate to position favorably vs competitors.

sources: SF Contact Role: Richard Morin, VP Corporate Controller
Demo call notes 21 May (Richard's SAP/matching questions)
SF Next Step 1 Jun, 18 Jun (Richard not mentioned in recent outreach, Neil is primary contact)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Richard Morin, VP Corporate Controller, is the confirmed Economic Buyer. He is an active participant in the evaluation: he asked commercially relevant questions during the 21 May demo (two-way vs three-way matching configuration, SAP integration partner level) and is a contact role on the opportunity. His title (VP Corporate Controller) and domain (AP, invoice processing, ERP integration) map directly to budget authority for an S2P platform purchase. However, his engagement has been passive (asking questions) rather than active sponsorship (advocating for Zycus, driving the timeline, or blocking competitors). No commercial or pricing conversation has occurred yet, and no executive sponsor above Richard has been identified. The mid-July buyer reconnect is the critical moment to move Richard from evaluator to active sponsor by presenting a quantified ROI case and requesting his advocacy in the down-select. If Richard is the approver, we need his explicit commitment; if he reports to a CFO or CEO for final sign-off, we need to map that approval chain now.

recommended_moves: During the expected mid-July buyer reconnect (target: 15-20 Jul), surface competitive intelligence: ask Neil who else is in the RFI, how many finalists Perdue expects to down-select, and how Zycus is currently rated vs the field. If Neil won't share, triangulate by asking what criteria Perdue is prioritizing and where Zycus needs to strengthen its position. Without this intelligence, Zycus is flying blind into the down-select.

Build and present a quantified ROI case to Richard Morin (VP Corporate Controller, Economic Buyer) during or immediately after the mid-July reconnect. Ask Richard or Neil to share current-state pain metrics (invoice volume, manual touch points, AP headcount, sourcing cycle time, error rate) so Zycus can model cost savings, cycle-time reduction, and headcount efficiency gains. Anchor the ROI case to Perdue's financial goals and position it as the business justification for the EB/CFO proposal.

Assess Neil Schloss's influence in the down-select during mid-July reconnect: does he have a vote? does he report to Richard Morin or a CPO? who else on the evaluation team could champion Zycus? If Neil is purely a process coordinator with limited scorecard influence, identify and cultivate a second advocate with decision weight, ideally someone who values Zycus's AI/Merlin differentiation or has a relationship from the past iContract deployment. Target: Christina Sandefur (Sourcing Manager) or Jennifer Langley (Director of Purchasing) as potential secondary champions.
